purpose: Column-aware natural language routing hints for ECM/DCM. Use this map to translate user criteria into deterministic SQL filters/groupings/orderings. Check fast_path_intents.json first — if a fast-path matches, skip entity_search. Check unsupported_intents.json for hard stops.
principles: Any listed column can be used as criteria when user intent maps to it.
Prefer exact code matching for coded fields; use LIKE/REGEXP only for free text.
When both deal-level and tranche-level fields are present, align business grain before aggregation.
Always apply PRODUCT and date predicates explicitly.
Fast-path: if ask contains ONLY taxonomy filters (sector/currency/region/product/date/status/category) and NO named entity, skip entity_search entirely.

Unsupported-path: if ask maps to peer companies, investor country, settlement currency, matched orders, TTW orders, or per-bank allocation — respond immediately with the unsupported message from unsupported_intents.json. NOTE: order type / away / OTT / scaled IS supported via ORDER_TYPE.

criteria_hints.sql_hint: WHERE PRODUCT = 'ECM' or PRODUCT = 'DCM'
Resolve by entity_search for name-based asks; use DEAL_ID when resolved. NEVER use DEAL_NAME LIKE in final SQL once DEAL_ID is known.

Tranche-level size stored as TEXT: use TO_NUMBER(TRANCHE_SIZE DEFAULT NULL ON CONVERSION ERROR) for comparisons/ranking/SUM. Dedupe by DEAL_ID/TRANCHE_ID first. For DEAL-level size use DEAL_SIZE (do NOT SUM tranche sizes).
Deal-level size (ECM+DCM). Use DEAL_SIZE directly — do NOT compute SUM(TRANCHE_SIZE). Dedupe by DEAL_ID before ranking/aggregating (the value repeats across flattened rows).
WHERE CURRENCY = 'USD' (or IN list). For multi-currency: COUNT(DISTINCT CURRENCY) > 1 per DEAL_ID. No settlement-currency or demand-currency column exists.
criteria_hints.unsupported_note: No SETTLEMENT_CURRENCY or DEMAND_CURRENCY column. CURRENCY is pricing currency only.

criteria_hints.sql_hint: WHERE SECTOR = '<normalized_value>'. Apply normalizations: defence->Aero/Defense, pharma->Pharmaceuticals, banking->Banks, it->Information Technology, telecom->Telecommunications, auto->Autos.
Use sargable date ranges; avoid TO_CHAR/EXTRACT/TRUNC predicates on PRICING_TS. Use ADD_MONTHS(SYSDATE,-12) for last 12 months. Q1=Jan-Mar, Q2=Apr-Jun, Q3=Jul-Sep, Q4=Oct-Dec.
Use SETTLEMENT_TS for settlement-specific time filters. NOTE: there is no SETTLEMENT_CURRENCY column — do not confuse settlement date with settlement currency.
criteria_hints.unsupported_note: No SETTLEMENT_CURRENCY column. Cannot filter by settlement currency vs deal currency.

criteria_hints.sql_hint: Tranche-level region; works for both ECM and DCM. Default to TRANCHE_REGION for generic 'region' asks unless the user explicitly says deal-level region (then DEAL_REGION, ECM-only).
criteria_hints.note: The old REGION column has been REMOVED from the view. Region is now TRANCHE_REGION (tranche-level, ECM+DCM) or DEAL_REGION (deal-level, ECM-only). Do NOT use TRANCHE_REGION/DEAL_REGION for INVESTOR region/geography asks (e.g. 'USA orders', 'US investors') — those are unsupported.

criteria_hints.sql_hint: Deal-level region. ECM-only (NULL for DCM). Use for explicit deal-level region asks; otherwise default to TRANCHE_REGION.
criteria_hints.note: Replaces the removed REGION column. DEAL_REGION is populated for ECM only.

criteria_hints.sql_hint: Investor-side metric (ECM+DCM): resolve investor to GPNUM and aggregate/filter ALLOCATION. Dedupe by GPNUM before SUM. For explicit 'demand' asks use DEMAND_QTY, not ALLOCATION.
criteria_hints.note: ALLOCATION is final confirmed allocation. 'indication shares' = SUM(ALLOCATION). 'demand' now maps to DEMAND_QTY.

criteria_hints.sql_hint: DCM investor-side amount metric; filter investor by GPNUM. For ECM 'order size' asks, use ALLOCATION instead — AMT is DCM-only. For explicit 'demand' asks use DEMAND_QTY.
criteria_hints.note: No ECM order-size column — use ALLOCATION for ECM investor ranking. 'demand' maps to DEMAND_QTY.

criteria_hints.sql_hint: Dedicated demand metric = Indication in shares (ECM+DCM). Use DEMAND_QTY whenever the user says 'demand' (or 'indication in shares'). Investor-side: filter investor by GPNUM; dedupe by GPNUM before SUM.

user_phrases: orders placed by other syndicate banks
criteria_hints.sql_hint: Order-handling type (ECM+DCM). WHERE UPPER(ORDER_TYPE) LIKE UPPER('%<value>%'). Sample values 'OTT','REGULAR','SCALED','LIMIT','MARKET','AWAY' — the list is INCOMPLETE, so use LIKE and do not reject other values. 'away orders' / 'orders placed by other banks' map here.
ECM: WHERE OFFERING_TYPE IN ('IPO','FO','Rights Issue','Block','Convertible'). DCM values: benchmark/tap issuance.

WHERE EQUITY_TYPE IN ('Convertible Bonds','Convertible Preferred','Common Stock','Warrants','Exchangable Notes'). ECM-only. Note: 'senior secured' for ECM should use EQUITY_TYPE for convertibles, not SENIORITY.

Finer-grained ECM security type (more specific than EQUITY_TYPE). ECM-only. WHERE UPPER(PRODUCT_TYPE) LIKE UPPER('%<value>%') or exact match. Examples: 'Common Stock', 'ADR', 'ADS', 'GDR', 'Rights', 'Mandatory Convertible Preferred Stock'. Use EQUITY_TYPE for coarse-grained asks.
criteria_hints.note: Free-text column; prefer LIKE for partial matches (e.g. 'ADR', 'depositary'). Distinct from EQUITY_TYPE.

criteria_hints.sql_hint: ECM listing exchange. ECM-only. WHERE UPPER(EXCHANGE) LIKE UPPER('%<value>%'). Examples: 'NASDAQ', 'New York Stock Exchange', 'London Stock Exchange'. Note NYSE appears as both 'New York Stock Exchange' and 'NYSE (New York Stock Exchange)' — use LIKE.
DCM-only: WHERE SENIORITY LIKE '%<value>%'. For ECM convertibles, use EQUITY_TYPE not SENIORITY.
criteria_hints.note: SENIORITY is DCM-only. For ECM 'convertibles', use EQUITY_TYPE. 'Senior secured' applies to DCM only.

criteria_hints.sql_hint: WHERE PRODUCT_CLASS = '<value>'. DCM-only column.
criteria_hints.note: Values are bond classes -> populated mainly for DCM; column exists on both products.

criteria_hints.sql_hint: WHERE REG_CATEGORY LIKE '%<value>%' or exact match. DCM-only.
DCM-specific legal delivery filter. WHERE DELIVERY_TYPE = '144A' etc.
WHERE ESG_BOND IN ('GREEN','SOCIAL','SUSTAINABILITY'). DCM-only.

WHERE INVESTOR_CATEGORY = '<value>'. Valid ECM values: Long Only, Hedge Fund, Outright, Long/Hedge, Outright/Hedge. Valid DCM values: Central Bank, Official Institution, Insurance/Pension, Asset Manager, Corporate Treasury, Bank Treasury, Private Bank, and others.

ECM-only. Use MEETING_TYPE_KEY: ONE_TO_ONE, CONFERENCE_CALL, SMALL_GROUP, GROUP_MEETING, NO_MEETING. For '1x1': MEETING_TYPE_KEY = 'ONE_TO_ONE'. For 'other than 1x1': MEETING_TYPE_KEY != 'ONE_TO_ONE'.
WHERE DEAL_STATUS = '<value>' (case-insensitive). For cancelled/deleted ORDER-level asks: NOT SUPPORTED — DEAL_STATUS is deal-level only.
criteria_hints.note: DEAL_STATUS is deal-level. No order-level cancellation/deletion status exists.

criteria_hints.sql_hint: ECM-only deal execution status category.
WHERE COUPON_TYPE = '<value>'. Exact literal for fixed-to-floating asks is 'Fixed-to-Floating'. DCM-only.
WHERE COUPON_FREQ = '<value>'. DCM-only.
SECURITIES_MATURITY is stored as TEXT: filter with LIKE on the year (e.g. LIKE '%2030%'); never compare against DATE literals. DCM-only.
WHERE TENORS LIKE '%<value>%'. DCM-only. Pipe-delimited if multiple.
WHERE USE_OF_PROCEEDS LIKE '%<value>%'. 'GCP' = literal 'General Corporate Purposes' (LIKE '%General Corporate%').
WHERE ISSUER_RATINGS LIKE '%<value>%'. DCM-only. Pipe-delimited multi-agency ratings.
Resolve by entity_search (issuer_name type), then WHERE GFCID = '<resolved>'. Never use ISSUER_NAME LIKE in final SQL once GFCID is known.
criteria_hints.note: GFCID = issuer identifier. Never use GFCID for investor filters.

criteria_hints.sql_hint: Resolve by entity_search (investor_name type), then WHERE GPNUM = '<resolved>'. Never use INVESTOR_NAME LIKE in final SQL once GPNUM is known.
criteria_hints.note: GPNUM = investor identifier. Never use GPNUM for issuer filters.

criteria_hints.sql_hint: DCM-only lowest grain. WHERE ORDER_ID = '<value>'.

Use branch-aware token matching. ECM: pipe-aligned lists with '(^| \| )' anchor. DCM: scalar BND_BROKER + SYNDICATE_MEMBER_NAME LIKE. For non-B&D: negate BND_BROKER TRUE set — do NOT call entity_search. For 'Citi billed'=B&D, 'non-Citi billed'=non-B&D.
criteria_hints.note: ALL broker/syndicate intents bypass entity_search entirely. 'non-B&D', 'not B&D', 'non bill and deliver' are SQL filter modifiers — not entity names.

criteria_hints.sql_hint: Include IDENTIFIER_TYPE, IDENTIFIER_VALUE, TICKER in SELECT when requested. Both ECM and DCM.
